name: Session Lifecycle Characterization — Cap State is Session-Scoped
details: Characterization: session lifecycle is session-scoped (NOT principal-scoped). Session A created→deleted cleanly; GET-after-delete returns ResourceNotFoundException (state purged) (normal AWS resource lifecycle). List-after-delete excludes A: True. Churn 5/5 cycles clean, no rate limit at this pace. New session B under same (userId, agentName) starts with full cap $0.10 — no carry-over from A. Documents that cap state is per-session-lifecycle; companion finding to ACP-001. NOT a vulnerability — operators relying on cumulative-across-sessions tracking must layer at application.

request_sent.audit_note_2026_05_26: Original PASS criterion punished normal AWS resource lifecycle (state purged on delete = correct behavior). Reframed as characterization; the security signal is the companion to ACP-001 (no principal-bound cap state).

csg_mapping: HC-2: Operators must track cumulative cap at application layer if principal-bound spend governance is required
